message.content:
This section (Page 4) details the quantitative performance, visual encoding, and methodological boundaries of the Chinese mobile gaming market, contextualizing the industry sentiment shift from “recovery anxiety” to “strategic dormancy.” 
- **Topics & Claims:** Tracks market revenue trends indicating a post-2021 decline, followed by gradual recovery and stable long-term growth. Claims highlight a growth rate peak in 2023 and a projected deceleration through 2028.
- **Figures & Visual Conventions:** Features a combined bar-and-line chart. Blue bars represent market revenue scale; cyan lines represent annual growth rates. A red dashed box annotation highlights key temporal/predictive points, specifically marking 2025 data. No standalone tables are present.
- **Metrics & Dates:** Covers annual data from 2021–2028. Key metrics include 2021 revenue (276.94 billion RMB), 2028 projected revenue (305.92 billion RMB), 2022 growth (3.21%), 2023 peak growth (10.74%), and 2028 projected growth (1.83%). Chart units are 100 million RMB (亿元) for revenue and percentage (%) for growth.
- **Definitions & Scope:** Market scope is restricted to mobile games launched in mainland China (excludes PC/console; multi-platform titles count only mobile data). Revenue scope covers total direct user spending, explicitly excluding ad monetization and third-party top-ups. This standard applies to all subsequent “China revenue” statistics.
- **Entities & Evidence Types:** Data sourced from 点点数据 (Diandian Data), corporate financial reports, expert interviews, and proprietary statistical modeling. Evidence comprises structured historical financial metrics and forward-looking predictive forecasts.
- **Procedures & Reporting:** Data is reported annually. A procedural note specifies that figures may be revised in future 2026 publications.

response_text:
This section (Page 4) details the quantitative performance, visual encoding, and methodological boundaries of the Chinese mobile gaming market, contextualizing the industry sentiment shift from “recovery anxiety” to “strategic dormancy.” 
- **Topics & Claims:** Tracks market revenue trends indicating a post-2021 decline, followed by gradual recovery and stable long-term growth. Claims highlight a growth rate peak in 2023 and a projected deceleration through 2028.
- **Figures & Visual Conventions:** Features a combined bar-and-line chart. Blue bars represent market revenue scale; cyan lines represent annual growth rates. A red dashed box annotation highlights key temporal/predictive points, specifically marking 2025 data. No standalone tables are present.
- **Metrics & Dates:** Covers annual data from 2021–2028. Key metrics include 2021 revenue (276.94 billion RMB), 2028 projected revenue (305.92 billion RMB), 2022 growth (3.21%), 2023 peak growth (10.74%), and 2028 projected growth (1.83%). Chart units are 100 million RMB (亿元) for revenue and percentage (%) for growth.
- **Definitions & Scope:** Market scope is restricted to mobile games launched in mainland China (excludes PC/console; multi-platform titles count only mobile data). Revenue scope covers total direct user spending, explicitly excluding ad monetization and third-party top-ups. This standard applies to all subsequent “China revenue” statistics.
- **Entities & Evidence Types:** Data sourced from 点点数据 (Diandian Data), corporate financial reports, expert interviews, and proprietary statistical modeling. Evidence comprises structured historical financial metrics and forward-looking predictive forecasts.
- **Procedures & Reporting:** Data is reported annually. A procedural note specifies that figures may be revised in future 2026 publications.
